Question:
We plan to conduct a machine learning analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or retinopathy status.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

Dataset: NHANES

Exclusion Criteria:
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- (Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes? = "borderline" AND (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) is missing OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%))
- serum albumin (g/dL) is missing
- diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy? is missing

Covariates:
- gender

- race/hispanic origin categorized as:
  White,

- serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles:
  Q1: <25%
  Q2: ≥25 and <50%
  Q3: ≥50 and <75%
  Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy? = "Yes"; 0 if diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy? = "No"'

Question:
We will use 2011-2014 NHANES data. The study will include people who are young adults, who has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following: who said "yes" or "borderline" to self-reported diabetes questionaire, reported taking diabetes medication, fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) >126
Exclude participants with missing values in adult food security questionaire and missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead".
The main predictor is food insecurity level defined baesd on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as severe food insecurity for participants who reported very low food security or low food security, mild food insecurity for participants who reported marginal food security, and food secure for who reported full food security.
we will do exam weighted logistic regression where outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

Inclusion Criteria:
- young adults
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - self-reported diabetes questionaire = "yes" OR "borderline"
    - reported taking diabetes medication
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
None

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if reported "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if reported "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if reported "full food security"

- depression outcome = 1 if clinically relevant depression; 0 otherwise
  Clinically relevant depression is defined as total score >= 10 from the nine depression questions:
  "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question value is null, assign 0.
  Total score = sum of scores from the nine questions.'

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2018. 
We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked about self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or the use of diabetes medication. 
We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).
We will exclude participants who are missing sex, race/ethnicity, urine albumin mg/L, or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
2. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
3. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
Adjust the analysis for race/ethnicity (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

we will do exam weighted logistic regression where outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score was categorized as binary to determine clinically relevant depression and else was not.

'Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - self-reported diabetes questionaire = "yes" OR self-reported diabetes questionaire = "borderline"
    OR
    - reported taking diabetes medication
    OR
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
None

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome (binary) = 1 if total score meets the threshold for clinically relevant depression; 0 otherwise
  Total score calculated by summing the values of the nine depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question's value mapping:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
  Note: Participants with missing values for any of these questions are excluded per exclusion criteria.
  Threshold for clinically relevant depression is not specified.'

The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".

urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

Question:
We plan to conduct a popular regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or retinopathy status.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

    Q3: ≥50% and <75%
    Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?” = "Yes"; 0 if “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?” = "No"'

The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was a long time ago, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or retinopathy status.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

    - More than half the days (2)
    - Nearly every day (3)
  If value is null, assign (0).'

We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

Question:
We will plan to do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights from NHANES data from 2007-2018. 
We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked about self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or the use of diabetes medication. 
We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).
We will exclude participants who are missing sex, race/ethnicity, urine albumin mg/L, or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). We will exclude all participants with an eGFR below 90.
The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
2. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
3. if gender is male and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
Adjust the analysis for race/ethnicity (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:

  - urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) calculated as:

The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or retinopathy status.
We will categorize serum albumin (g/dL), into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).
We will exclude participants who are missing sex, race/ethnicity, urine albumin mg/L, or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). We will exclude participants who have recorded values for waist circumference (cm).
The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159

urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
Adjust the analysis for race/ethnicity (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

'Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as meeting any of the following:
    - self-reported diabetes questionaire = "yes" OR "borderline"
    - reported taking diabetes medication
    - fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the following depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
None

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "very low food security" or "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question reported "full food security"

Outcome:

    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question is scored as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0 (Note: Due to exclusion criteria, included participants will not have null values for these questions).'

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

- age at screening >= 20

    - reported taking diabetes medication

- missing adult food security questionaire

    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"

- food insecurity level categorized as:
    - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "very low food security" OR "low food security"
    - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question reported "marginal food security"
    - food secure: if adult food security question reported "full food security"

- depression outcome = 1 if total score from nine depression questions >= 10; 0 otherwise

    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"

    - "poor appetite or overeating"

    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question value mapped as:
    - Not at all: 0
    - Several days: 1
    - More than half the days: 2
    - Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0 for that question in the total score calculation.'

'Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

- reported yes when asked about self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes OR
- fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) >= 7.0 mmol/L OR

- use of diabetes medication

- pregnant at the exam

- missing waist circumference (cm)

- gender

    <60
    >=60

- weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio calculated as:
    waist circumference (cm) / sqrt(weight(kg))
  categorized as quartiles:
    Q1: <=25%
    Q2: >25% and <=50%
    Q3: >50% and <=75%
    Q4: >75%

- presence of kidney disease = 1 if (1) or (2) is met; 0 otherwise
  (1) estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) < 60 mL/min/1.73m^2, where eGFR is calculated as:
    If gender = Female AND race/hispanic origin = Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
    If gender = Female AND race/hispanic origin != Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
    If gender = Male AND race/hispanic origin = Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
    If gender = Male AND race/hispanic origin != Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
  OR
  (2) urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) >= 30 mg/g, where UACR (mg/g) is calculated as:
    100 * (urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL)'

urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/hispanic origin information for 4 different cases:

urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).

We will plan to do an analysis using dietary weights from NHANES data from 2007-2018. 
We will include participants who has diabetes when diabetes is defined as meeting any of the following requirements: reported yes when asked about self-reported doctor diagnosis of diabetes, or fasting plasma glucose (FPG, mmol/L) ≥7.0 mmol/L, or glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) ≥6.5%, or the use of diabetes medication. 
We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 20, pregnant at the exam, and had missing values of weight (kg), waist circumference (cm).
We will exclude participants who are missing sex, race/ethnicity, urine albumin mg/L, or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/hispanic origin information for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159

4. if gender is male and race/hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
Adjust the analysis for race/ethnicity (white and non white), gender, and age at screening (<60 and >=60).

Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or retinopathy status or serum albumini (g/dL) seems a little off.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with did not provide enough information.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other.

We will exclude participants who are missing sex, race/ethnicity, urine albumin mg/L, or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl). 
The predictor is weight-adjusted-waist (wwi) ratio which is calculated as waist circumference (cm) divided by the square root of weight(kg), categorized as quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater.

'Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- has diabetes, defined as extremely high blood sugar

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing values in adult food security questionaire
- missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead"

None

- food insecurity level defined based on the answer to the adult food security question, categorized as:

  - mild food insecurity: if reported "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if reported "full food security"

- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise

  Each question's value is mapped as:
    Not at all: 0
    Several days: 1
    More than half the days: 2
    Nearly every day: 3
  If a question's value is null, assign 0.'

'Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2014

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20
- NOT (
    self-reported diabetes questionaire = "yes" OR
    self-reported diabetes questionaire = "borderline" OR
    reported taking diabetes medication = TRUE OR
    fasting plasma glucose (mg/dl) > 126
  )

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing adult food security questionaire
- missing "have little interest in doing things"
- missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
- missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
- missing "feeling tired or having little energy"
- missing "poor appetite or overeating"
- missing "feeling bad about yourself"
- missing "trouble concentrating on things"
- missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
- missing "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates: None

Predictor:
- food insecurity level categorized as:
  - severe food insecurity: if adult food security question = "very low food security" OR adult food security question = "low food security"
  - mild food insecurity: if adult food security question = "marginal food security"
  - food secure: if adult food security question = "full food security"

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  - Total score calculated as the sum of scores from the nine depression questions:
    - "have little interest in doing things"
    - "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    - "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    - "feeling tired or having little energy"
    - "poor appetite or overeating"
    - "feeling bad about yourself"
    - "trouble concentrating on things"
    - "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    - "thought you would be better off dead"
  - Each question's value mapping:
    - Not at all = 0
    - Several days = 1
    - More than half the days = 2
    - Nearly every day = 3
    - If value is null, assign 0'
